summary: Unmerged cleanup/decomp slice titled for Crazy Hand. The PR was closed almost immediately because the author said it was meant to be opened against their fork, so there is no reviewer validation. The diff was very large: 103 files, with major work in ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c plus unrelated or supporting changes in common fighter code, camera, lbaudio, menus, stages, items, headers, CI, and configure.py. Evidence shows many placeholder `/// #...` functions being replaced with C implementations, prototype/header churn, and build/prototype enforcement tweaks.
what_changed: Opened as PR #2064 but closed within seconds; the only human comment says: "sorry, meant to create this against my fork again".
Large-scale code cleanup/decomp attempt across 103 files, adding 6675 lines and deleting 1450 lines according to the PR context.

The largest file change was `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c` (+983/-651), replacing many `/// #...` placeholders with C implementations for Crazy Hand-related action logic such as `ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B174`, `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`, `ftCh_Damage2_Phys`, `ftCh_Slap_Anim`, `ftCh_WalkLoop_Anim`, `ftCh_RockCrushDown_Phys`, `ftCh_Poke1_Anim`, and others visible in the diff excerpt.

`ftCh_Init.c` added many direct includes for common fighter, boss, item, audio, and collision/support helpers, including `ftCo_CaptureCut.h`, `ftCo_Throw.h`, `ftCo_Thrown.h`, `ft_0877.h`, `ft_0881.h`, `ftanim.h`, `ftcommon.h`, `itmasterhandlaser.h`, `lb_00B0.h`, `lbaudio_ax.h`, and `mplib.h`.

`ftCh_Init.c` removed a large inline `MotionState ftCh_Init_MotionStateTable[ftCh_MS_SelfCount]` block from this file and replaced that section with forward/static-style declarations plus the existing Crazy Hand data-name strings. The destination of the table is not shown in the excerpt, so this should be treated as observed deletion from this file, not a confirmed relocation.

`src/melee/cm/camera.c` received several large decompiled implementations replacing placeholders, including `Camera_8002CB0C`, `Camera_8002CDDC`, `Camera_8002D85C`, and a partial/excerpted `Camera_80030E44` area.

Camera code added `gr/grlib.h` and used helpers such as `Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset`, `Stage_GetPauseCamZPosInit`, `Stage_GetCamAngleRadiansUp/Down/Left/Right`, `Stage_GetCamTrackSmooth`, `Player_GetEntity`, and `ftLib_80086B74` in pause-camera and tracking logic.

`src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c` received a large decompilation expansion (+795/-98), with many placeholder removals for item scope, catch, capture, and related common fighter routines visible in grep output.
`src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c` and `src/melee/cm/camera.c` were among the largest non-Crazy-Hand changes, suggesting this PR was broader than the title.
`configure.py` changed `--require-protos` to default on and added `--no-require-protos` as an explicit opt-out.

The GitHub workflow switched build images from `doldecomp/build-melee:main` to `itsgrimetime/build-melee:main`, removed repository guards, and stopped passing `--require-protos` explicitly in one path, consistent with the PR being intended for a fork rather than upstream.

The diff added a symlink-like `orig/GALE01/sys` pointing to `/tmp/melee_extract/sys` and deleted `orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep`, another sign that the PR carried local/fork setup changes not suitable for upstream as-is.
smart_moves: Prototype enforcement was made the default in `configure.py` while still adding `--no-require-protos` for cases that need to bypass it; this is a reusable build-system pattern, though this PR was not reviewed or merged.

Several matching-oriented C implementations preserve control-flow shape with explicit `goto` labels, especially in the camera pause/slot-selection routines. This is a common tactic when matching generated PowerPC control flow.
The camera implementations use concrete game helper APIs instead of open-coded assumptions: `Player_GetEntity`, `ftLib_80086B74`, stage camera-bound accessors, and vector helpers appear repeatedly.
The diff shows careful stack/layout nudges such as `PAD_STACK` changes in camera and effect code, indicating attention to ABI/stack matching rather than only functional readability.

The Crazy Hand code replaces float extern constants in at least one motion-state call with literal `0.0f`/`1.0f`, which may simplify matching if the original function used immediate float constants; however, no reviewer confirmation is available.
decomp_lessons: Do not treat this PR as accepted upstream knowledge: it was closed because it was opened against the wrong repository target and received no review comments.

Large cleanup PRs can accidentally carry fork/local infrastructure changes; future decomp submissions should separate source matching work from CI image changes, local `orig` symlinks, and repository-specific workflow edits.

When introducing default prototype enforcement, provide an explicit opt-out flag rather than silently weakening checks. This PR’s `--require-protos`/`--no-require-protos` change is a useful pattern if separately validated.
For matching control-flow-heavy camera routines, preserving branch structure with labels and `goto` may be more practical than forcing structured C too early.

Boss/fighter action-state work often needs broad includes across common fighter actions, item helpers, audio, collision, and boss libraries; this PR demonstrates that Crazy Hand cleanup was not isolated to only `ftCrazyHand` headers.

If moving or deleting large data tables such as a fighter `MotionState` table, make the relocation explicit in reviewable chunks. The excerpt proves deletion from `ftCh_Init.c` but not where the table ended up.
Unreviewed mass placeholder replacement should be mined only for tactics and search terms, not for final symbol names or correctness.
naming_conventions: Crazy Hand symbols use the `ftCh_` prefix, e.g. `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`, `ftCh_Slap_Phys`, `ftCh_WalkLoop_Anim`, and `ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B174`.
Master Hand/Crazy Hand motion IDs in comments and enum-style names use `ftMh_MS_*` and `ftMh_SM_*`, visible in the removed motion-state table comments.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic functions retain address-based names such as `fn_80159908`, `fn_80157080`, `ftCh_Init_80159F40`, and `Camera_8002CB0C`.
Static-only declarations were written in the style `/* static */ void ...`, preserving uncertainty or matching constraints about linkage.
Common fighter symbols use `ftCo_` prefixes, while library and camera routines use `lb*` and `Camera_800xxxxx` address naming respectively.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Uses explicit `goto` labels such as `loop_top`, `loop_body`, `fallback_path`, `check_done`, and `skip_fov_calc` in camera functions to preserve branch layout.
Uses `PAD_STACK` adjustments, including a camera stack pad change from `PAD_STACK(8)` to `PAD_STACK(12)` and effect-code pad changes visible in the diff excerpt/search output.
Camera input bit tests use 64-bit union-style access like `inputs.x18._u64 & 0x20ULL` and `inputs.x10._u64 & 0x400ULL`, likely to match load/test width.
Motion-state transitions are expressed with exact flag constants such as `0x0C4C5280`, `0x02000000`, and `0x0c4c5080` in common fighter code.

Crazy Hand and camera implementations frequently use literal float constants such as `0.0f`, `1.0f`, `0.5f`, `3.0f`, `20.0f`, `30.0f`, and `45.0f`, matching observed game thresholds and motion arguments in the diff.
Struct-field uncertainty is preserved with address-based or opaque fields such as `fp->mv.mh.unk0.x0`, `camera->x2C4`, `camera->x2C5`, `cm_803BCCA0.x40`, and `cm_80452C68`.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.text: sorry, meant to create this against my fork again
No review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR context.

follow_up_queries: Find whether the Crazy Hand motion-state table was moved to another file in later accepted PRs.
Search later PRs for accepted implementations of `ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B174`, `ftCh_Damage2_Anim`, and related Crazy Hand routines.
Search accepted camera PRs for `Camera_8002CB0C`, `Camera_8002CDDC`, `Camera_8002D85C`, and the same pause-camera control-flow patterns.
Check whether `--require-protos` default-on with `--no-require-protos` was later merged or changed independently.
Compare later upstream workflow files to ensure fork-local image changes from this accidental PR were not reused.
